data.title: Iran Warns Against U.S. Naval Blockade
data.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Baghaei stated on April 17 that a U.S. naval blockade against Iran would breach the ceasefire agreement. According to NS3.AI, Baghaei emphasized that Tehran is prepared to implement countermeasures if required.

data.title: Figure Counters Allegations of Blockchain Misrepresentation
data.description: Figure has responded to claims made by Morpheus Research, which suggested that Figure-related entities control over 65% of the HASH token on the Provenance Blockchain and that the company exaggerates its blockchain usage. According to NS3.AI, Figure clarified that funded loans, ownership transfers, and pledges are indeed recorded and executed on-chain. The company highlighted a 0.80% weighted-average delinquency rate across approximately $4.6 billion in securitized assets. Additionally, Figure reported executing over $1.15 billion in whole loan sales on its marketplace in March 2026.

data.title: World ID Protocol Receives Major Upgrade with Global Expansion
data.description:
World announced a significant upgrade to its World ID protocol on April 18, expanding its reach to approximately 18 million users verified by Orb across more than 160 countries. According to BlockBeats, this update is considered the most important iteration to date, introducing several practical applications. The upgrade integrates with Tinder to provide 'real person certification' for enhancing authenticity in dating, incorporates a deepfake verification feature with Zoom to confirm call participants as real humans, and launches the Concert Kit ticketing tool for identity verification and anti-scalping measures.

Additionally, World has released a standalone World ID app (beta version) as a cross-platform identity authentication tool and has open-sourced its SDK, allowing third-party applications to build their own authentication systems.

On the technical front, the upgrade introduces multi-key management, key rotation, account recovery, and session management mechanisms. It also proposes the concept of 'Human Continuity,' emphasizing continuous verification of the same real individual across multiple interactions, rather than just verifying devices or accounts.

In terms of ecosystem development, Reddit is exploring the use of World ID for identifying automated accounts, while Razer and Mythical Games have already adopted the standard in gaming scenarios.

Regarding the business model, World ID will charge fees to applications based on active users, while the user side remains free. Fees can be settled through on-chain wallets or third-party services, with support for automatic protocol-level distribution.

data.title: Alcoa in Talks to Sell New York Site to Bitcoin Mining Firm NYDIG
data.description: Alcoa is reportedly in advanced discussions to sell a former smelter site located in upper New York state to the bitcoin mining company NYDIG. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Alcoa's strategy to divest its dormant assets as part of its broader business plan. The potential sale underscores the growing interest in repurposing industrial sites for cryptocurrency mining operations. This move aligns with Alcoa's efforts to streamline its operations and focus on its core business areas. Details of the transaction, including the financial terms, have not been disclosed.

data.title: Trump said a deal between the US and Iran could be reached "within a day or two".
data.description: US President Trump stated that US-Iran talks are expected to take place this weekend, adding that the two sides "could reach an agreement within a day or two." In a phone interview, Trump expressed optimism about the progress of the negotiations, saying Iran "wants to meet and reach an agreement." He also emphasized that the naval blockade against Iran would not be lifted until a formal agreement is reached, but he hoped the Strait of Hormuz would be open to all countries. It is understood that although significant progress has been made in the negotiations, differences remain on key issues, including the suspension period for Iran's nuclear enrichment and the handling of its enriched uranium stockpile. The agreement also includes the possibility that the US might unfreeze approximately $20 billion in Iranian assets in exchange for Iran relinquishing its highly enriched uranium reserves. Furthermore, Trump stated that the agreement would "make Israel safer" and demanded that Israel cease its military strikes against Lebanon. Iran has not yet responded to these statements. (CCTV News)

data.title: Wall Street Firms Expand Digital Asset Access with New Bitcoin ETFs
data.description: Goldman Sachs has submitted an application for a Bitcoin income ETF, marking a significant move in the digital asset space. According to NS3.AI, this development comes as Morgan Stanley's recently launched Bitcoin ETF achieved a milestone, reaching $100 million in its first week. Additionally, Charles Schwab has announced that its clients can now trade Bitcoin and Ether within their accounts. These actions highlight the growing interest and involvement of major Wall Street firms in expanding access to digital assets.

data.title: HSBC Executive Departs Amidst Wave of Exits in Middle East Division
data.description: The head of HSBC's newly established banking division for the Middle East, North Africa, and Turkey has departed, according to sources familiar with the situation. Bloomberg posted on X that this departure is part of a series of recent exits at the bank. The reasons behind these departures have not been disclosed, and HSBC has yet to comment on the situation. The bank's Middle East division has been undergoing significant changes as it seeks to strengthen its presence in the region. The departure of key personnel could impact the bank's strategic plans and operations in these markets.

data.title: The UN Secretary-General called for the full restoration of navigation rights and freedoms in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres, through his spokesperson, issued a statement welcoming Iran's announcement of the full opening of the Strait of Hormuz for commercial vessels during the ceasefire period, and called on all parties to fully restore and respect international navigation rights and freedoms in the strait. The statement said Guterres welcomed Iran's announcement that day that it would fully open the Strait of Hormuz to all commercial vessels for the remainder of the ceasefire, considering this move "a step in the right direction." Guterres reiterated that the UN will continue to fully support finding a peaceful solution to the current Middle East conflict through diplomatic means. He expressed hope that, based on the ceasefire arrangement, this measure would help enhance mutual trust among all parties and promote progress in the dialogue process mediated by Pakistan. (CCTV News)

data.title: Representative Sheri Biggs Reveals Significant Bitcoin Investment
data.description: Representative Sheri Biggs has disclosed a substantial investment in Bitcoin, with exposure of up to $250,000 through the iShares Bitcoin Trust. According to NS3.AI, the House Periodic Transaction Report indicates that the transaction, valued between $100,001 and $250,000, was executed on March 4, 2026. This report was filed in mid-April, adhering to the STOCK Act deadlines. Biggs joins Senator David McCormick and Representative Brandon Gill as lawmakers who have reported significant Bitcoin ETF purchases over the past year.

data.title: Large USDT Transfer to Aave Raises Market Interest
data.description: A significant transfer of 290 million USDT was made from an unidentified wallet to Aave, valued at approximately $290 million. According to NS3.AI, this transaction has captured the attention of market participants, sparking discussions about potential impacts on liquidity and market dynamics. The movement of such a substantial amount of USDT into Aave could influence lending rates and borrowing activities within the platform, as well as broader market sentiment. Observers are closely monitoring the situation to assess any further developments or shifts in investor behavior.

data.title: European Coalition Offers Military Support for Strait of Hormuz Security
data.description: A European-led coalition has proposed military assistance to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X that this offer comes shortly after Iran announced its intention to open the vital waterway. Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump has cautioned NATO members to maintain their distance from the situation.

data.title: IMF Chief Warns of Rising Daily Risks Impacting Growth and Inflation
data.description: International Monetary Fund (IMF) Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva has expressed concerns about the increasing risks faced on a daily basis. According to Jin10, Georgieva highlighted the potential for these risks to lead to a scenario characterized by lower economic growth and higher inflation. Her remarks underscore the challenges that global economies may encounter as they navigate these uncertainties.

data.title: Binance Life's market capitalization surpasses $410 million, setting a new high in its rebound.
data.description: According to GMGN data, Binance Life's market capitalization has surpassed $410 million, setting a new high for the rebound, currently trading at $400 million, with a daily increase of over 13%. Odaily reminds users that the price of Meme coin is highly volatile, and investors should participate with caution.

data.title: RAVE broke through $22.66, hitting a new all-time high, with a daily increase of over 35%.
data.description: OKX data shows that RAVE has broken through $22.66, setting a new all-time high, and is currently trading at $22, with a daily increase of over 35%.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Daly Comments on Future Interest Rate Path
data.description: Federal Reserve official Daly has stated that the future trajectory of interest rates will depend on the duration of ongoing conflicts. According to ChainCatcher, Daly mentioned that if the conflicts conclude swiftly, the policy will revert to the previous interest rate path.

data.title: Inside the Coffee Grading Room: Setting Global Standards
data.description: Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, offering a glimpse into the unique process of coffee grading that sets global benchmarks. Expert graders meticulously evaluate coffee by sniffing, slurping, and spitting to determine its quality. This rigorous process ensures consistency and high standards in the coffee industry, impacting prices and consumer choices worldwide. The graders' expertise plays a crucial role in maintaining the integrity of coffee quality, influencing market dynamics and consumer preferences.

data.title: Fed's Daly: Interest rates may remain unchanged
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Daly indicated that the Fed may keep interest rates unchanged; if inflation accelerates, rates will need to be raised; and if the conflict ends quickly, rates may be cut. Maintaining the current policy will continue to curb inflation, medium- to long-term inflation expectations are stable, and as bank capital requirements change, reserve demand may decrease, potentially shrinking the Fed's balance sheet. (Jinshi)

data.title: The NYSE has submitted a proposal to amend its rules to allow tokenized securities to be listed and traded.
data.description: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) has submitted a rule amendment proposal to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), proposing a new Rule 7.50 and revising related provisions to allow eligible securities to be traded on the exchange in tokenized form. This proposal builds upon the Depository Trust Company (DTC) three-year tokenization pilot program and is modeled after similar rules previously approved by the SEC for Nasdaq. According to the proposal, tokenized securities must share the same CUSIP number, trading symbol, and shareholder rights as traditional securities to be traded with equal priority in the same order book. Initially, the application will be limited to Russell 1000 index constituents and ETFs tracking major indices, with the settlement cycle remaining T+1. Existing regulatory rules will also apply to tokenized securities.

data.title: Quantum Blockchain Raises Funds for AI-Driven Bitcoin Mining
data.description: Quantum Blockchain, a UK-listed company, has announced a placement of 142,857,142 new shares at a price of 0.35 pence per share, raising £500,000. According to Odaily, the company also revealed the establishment of a new Bitcoin mining subsidiary, BlocKeeper. The newly acquired funds will be directed towards accelerating the development of its AI-driven Bitcoin mining technology.

data.title: ORDI Experiences Significant Price Drop
data.description: ORDI's price briefly fell below 6 USDT, reaching a low of 5.818 USDT, according to Foresight News. It is currently reported at 6.077 USDT, marking a decline of approximately 25.74% over the past 24 hours.
